stories.title: Tusk accuses president of medal blunder as Poland seeks to keep influence in Ukraine war
stories.summary: WARSAW — Poland’s center-right government is accusing nationalist President Karol Nawrocki of making a strategic blunder by stripping Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Warsaw’s most prestigious medal : the Order of the White Eagle. Nawrocki withdrew the award late on Friday to protest the naming of a Ukrainian military unit last month after a World War II-era force that massacred tens of thousands of Poles. For the government of Prime Minister Donald Tusk, the priority is to

stories.title: Magyar unveils reform plans to write new constitution, oust top Orbán appointees
stories.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar unveiled plans Monday to write a new constitution and oust several of Hungary’s top officials from his predecessor Viktor Orbán’s administration — including President Tamás Sulyok. In a nearly hour-long speech to parliament, Magyar announced “Operation Purifying Fire” to root out what he called the “Orbán mafia.” The proposed reforms could result in the removal of Sulyok, as well as the head of the country’s Supreme Court

stories.title: Ex-DUP chief found guilty of sexual abuse in Northern Ireland
stories.summary: NEWRY, Northern Ireland — The disgraced former leader of Northern Ireland’s Democratic Unionist Party, Jeffrey Donaldson, was found guilty Monday of committing a 23-year series of sexual crimes against two young victims. After three days of deliberations, a jury found Donaldson, 63, guilty on all 18 criminal counts that he had sexually abused two women when they were children. The charges included 13 counts of indecent assault, four of gross indecency, and one of rape. The jurors also found that

stories.title: UK left in limbo as Starmer faces his lame duck era
stories.summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer’s plans to cement his legacy have been thrown into disarray, with promises to bolster defense spending and reset EU relations now looking highly uncertain as he enters a transition period before handing off to another Labour leader. The outgoing British prime minister will travel to international engagements with the responsibility of office but none of the power, while a question mark hangs over the intentions of his likely successor, Andy Burnham. Starmer announced Monday

stories.title: Weather tracker: Extreme heat in Europe as typhoon strengthens off Philippines
stories.summary: Eastern Europe to see temperatures above early July average into next week The extreme heat experienced across central and western Europe, including the UK, will continue to shift eastwards. As slightly cooler weather infiltrates into western Europe, with risks of downpours and thunderstorms, eastern Europe is likely to see temperatures several degrees above the early July average into next week. Peak highs of between 35C and 40C are expected across southern Poland, Slovakia, Hungary and into th

stories.title: Forget closer EU ties if Tories can’t face up to Brexit truth | Letters
stories.summary: Dr Denis MacShane says Labour figures were willing to break ranks over Europe, and implores Conservatives to tell the truth about Brexit. Plus a letter from Zhengli Zhou Timothy Garton-Ash is right to be worried that Brexit Britain’s political class is now monolingual, with very little personal contact with deciders in the EU ( If the UK wants to rejoin the European Union, it first needs to understand it, 15 June ). He lists pro-EU forces in politics and polls, but it is an error to assume that

stories.title: Magyar announces "Operation Cleansing Fire"
stories.summary: The comprehensive political and legal effort is aimed at freeing Hungary from the grip of corruption according to the PM, who said that since his government has taken office, they keep finding more and more overpriced contracts and concessions the previous administration left behind.

stories.title: Mém lett abból, ahogy a FIFA tűzzel-vassal irtja a nemkívánatos márkákat a foci-vb-n
stories.summary: Még a ketchupos üvegeket is leragasztották fekete szalaggal, hogy ne látszódjon a márkájuk.

stories.title: Olyan kevés víz van a Velencei-tóban, hogy a Magyar Kerékpárosklub elnöke konkrétan átsétálta
stories.summary: „Az iszapban járás kimerítő, és nem is túl gusztusos” – írta a tó átsétálásáról Kürti Gábor.

stories.title: Moving on from the sins of the Orbán regime too quickly would serve the interests of the perpetrators – Péter Krekó
stories.summary: More and more former servants of the Orbán system have been speaking out, but we haven’t heard nearly enough about the victims’ perspective, according to Péter Krekó. An interview with the political and behavioural scientist about the importance of Hungarian society coming to terms with the trauma of the past sixteen years.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a maffiáról és a Tisztítótűz-műveletről beszélt a rendkívüli parlamenti ülésen
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök átfogó akciót hirdetett „a politikai és gazdasági maffia” felszámolására. Az Alaptörvény módosításával elmozdítanák Sulyok Tamást és Polt Pétert is. Gulyás Gergely szerint Magyar nem maffiával küzd, hanem diktatúrát épít. Napirend előttiek, percről percre.

stories.title: Kifizetné a „szivárványos rongyok” eltávolítása miatti bírságot Novák Előd
stories.summary: A Mi Hazánk mellett a Hatvannégy Vármegye Ifjúsági Mozgalom is felajánlotta segítségét a Pride-zászlókat a Dunába dobáló ismeretlennek.

stories.title: Megpróbálta megmagyarázni a lengyel elnök hivatala, miért vonták vissza Zelenszkij kitüntetését, ha Mussolininál ezt nem tették meg
stories.summary: Támogatjuk Ukrajnát, de nem hagyjuk, hogy megsértsenek minket, nyilatkozta Karol Nawrocki hivatalának minisztere.

stories.title: Jön az Alaptörvény-módosítás, ami elmozdítja Sulyok Tamást a pozíciójából
stories.summary: Ezt Magyar Péter jelentette be a hétfői, napirend előtti felszólalásában a parlamentben.

stories.title: 70 éves korlimit az alkotmánybíróknak, 12 éves mandátumlimit a képviselőknek – ezeket jelentette be Magyar Péter a parlamentben
stories.summary: A Tisztítótűz művelet részeként Sulyok Tamás elmozdítását is kezdeményezi a kormány.

stories.title: A Fidesz közel a frakciója felét elveszítené, ha életbe lépne a Tisza 12 éves képviselői mandátum-korlátozása
stories.summary: A Tisztítótűz művelet értelmében Szijjártónak, Lázárnak, Gulyásnak és Kocsisnak is búcsúznia kellene a parlamenttől, míg a 70 éves korlimit négy alkotmánybírót érintene.

stories.title: Magyar röviden ismertette, hogyan működik majd a Nemzeti Vagyonvisszaszerzési és Védelmi Hivatal
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint akár az MNB-alapítványok ügyét is magához vonhatja majd a hivatal más hatóságoktól.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Sulyok Tamás egy hónap múlva már nem lesz Magyarország köztársasági elnöke
stories.summary: Ha Sulyok nem írja alá az Alaptörvény módosítását, kezdeményezik a megfosztási eljárást vele szemben, és az eljárás alatt már az Országgyűlés elnöke gyakorolja a jogait. A miniszterelnök szerint augusztus 20-a előtt megválasztja az Országgyűlés az új köztársasági elnököt, és úgy tudja, hogy a legfőbb ügyész hamarosan lemond.

stories.title: Egy művész sötét, dohos barlanggá alakította Párizs legrégebbi hídját
stories.summary: Aki végigsétál a Szajna feletti monumentális installáción, pár percre elfeledi, hogy a világ legforgalmasabb városának szívében jár.

stories.title: 44 nagykövetség és kulturális intézet állt ki a magyarországi Pride mellett
stories.summary: A külföldi képviseletek üdvözölik az új kormány kijelentését is, hogy Magyarországon mindenki úgy érezheti, hogy helye van a magyar nemzetben.

stories.title: A vízminőség alapján nem Albániában érdemes strandolni
stories.summary: Bár az ország egyre felkapottabb turisztikai célpont, a strandok vízminőségével komoly gondok vannak. Cipruson és Szlovéniában a legjobb a helyzet a magyarok által is kedvelt nyaralóhelyek közül.

stories.title: Karácsonyék néhány kivétellel minden közterületi hirdetést betiltanának, az összes budapesti óriásplakát lekerülhet
stories.summary: Hirdetőoszlopok csak néhány helyen, tetőreklámokból pedig csak néhány típus maradhatna Budapesten a Főpolgármesteri Hivatal rendelettervezete szerint. Az egyik érintett cég szerint ez értékrombolás és a fővárosnak is komoly bevételkiesést jelent.

stories.title: Észak királya – Keir Starmer az ő miniszterelnöksége útjából állt félre
stories.summary: Egy időközi választást megnyerve lett parlamenti képviselő Manchester polgármestere, akit messiásként vártak a brit Munkáspártban. Andy Burnham és gondosan válogatott ruhatára most az átlagemberek érdekeit képviselő, elitellenes politikusként visszatért, hogy átvegye az Egyesült Királyság irányítását.

stories.title: Magyar Péter első EU-csúcsának tanulságai
stories.summary: Hogy állt helyt Magyar Péter, aki elmondta, hogy nem akar közönségkedvenc lenni? Mit szóltak az EU-s vezetők ahhoz, hogy Orbán Viktor 16 év után nem volt jelen? Hogyan sikerült elcsípni egy kérdésre Emmanuel Macron francia elnököt? Az EU-csúcs tanulságait a helyszínről követő kollégáinkkal, Márton Balázzsal és Simor Dániellel elemeztük.

stories.title: Forró hangulat a Parlamentben: Magyar Péter meghirdette a Tisztítótűz műveletet, hogy leszámoljon az „orbáni maffiával”
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök rendesen roastolta az ellenzéket, azonban a fideszesek sem voltak begyulladva, visszavágtak Magyar Péternek, de ez csak olaj volt a tűzre.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely szerint a 12 évre szóló parlamenti mandátumkorlátozás nem visszamenőleges hatályú
stories.summary: A Fidesz frakcióvezetője viszont Magyar Péter beszéde miatt arra számít, hogy a Tisza módosító indítvánnyal változtathat ezen.

stories.title: Ha létezik, ha nem, politikai szellemként kering a nyilvánosságban Zsolti bácsi
stories.summary: Noha már a kampányban sem tudta bizonyítani senki „Zsolti bácsi” létezését, a titokzatos figura a közélet része lett. A kampányban a Fideszre, most viszont Magyar Péterre lőnek vele, más-más okból. Gyorstalpaló.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint nemcsak a fideszesek, a tiszások miatt is fontos lesz a vagyonvédelmi hivatal
stories.summary: A kormányfő ATV-nek adott interjújában a tisztázatlan körülmények között meghalt felcsúti juhász esetének kivizsgálása is szóba került.

stories.title: Orbánék tétlenkedése miatt összezsugorodtak a napelemes és egészségügyi célok, Magyarék a zöld közlekedésre gyúrnak
stories.summary: Nyilvánossá vált a 10 milliárd eurót érő uniós helyreállítási alaphoz beadott magyar terv jóváhagyott módosítása. Ennek beadására azért volt szüksége a Tisza-kormánynak, mert az Orbán-kormány rosszul haladt a saját vállalásaival. Megnéztük, mi változott.

stories.title: Visszahelyezték posztjára a tavaly leváltott mentős igazgatót
stories.summary: Leváltása után Magyar Péter posztban ígérte meg, hogy majd újra vezetőként számítanak rá, mivel eltávolítása „nyilvánvalóan politikai okokból” történt.

stories.title: Az elromlott klíma és a hőhullám miatt át kellett szervezni az Országos Onkológiai Intézet műtéti rendjét
stories.summary: A műtők egy részében nem biztosíthatók folyamatosan a megkövetelt feltételek.

stories.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
stories.summary: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

stories.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
stories.summary: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
stories.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

stories.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the wo

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
stories.summary: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: AI models that can take down governments and business months away, rare Five Eyes statement warns
stories.summary: Signal agencies in Australia, the US, the UK, New Zealand and Canada sound alarm after Trump blocks foreign nationals from Anthropic’s Fable AI model Powerful AI models capable of taking down governments and businesses are mere months away, cyber intelligence agencies for the Five Eyes have warned in a rare joint statement, urging leaders to “act now”. The surprising public intervention by signals agencies for Australia, the US, the UK, New Zealand and Canada comes after the Trump administration

stories.title: Iran agrees to UN nuclear inspectors’ return as part of agreement with US
stories.summary: Other measures include Washington lifting sanctions on Tehran’s oil exports and reopening the strait of Hormuz Middle East crisis live – latest updates Iran has agreed to allow UN nuclear inspectors back into the country as part of an agreement under which Washington will lift sanctions on Tehran’s oil exports and the strait of Hormuz will reopen, the US vice-president, JD Vance, has said. Long-term independent monitoring of Iran’s nuclear programme, which it says is for energy purposes only, wa

stories.title: New York City House primary emerges as key battleground in ‘AI civil war’
stories.summary: AI-focused Super Pacs are spending heavily in the midterms, and half has gone to a single Manhattan congressional race The artificial intelligence industry is spending heavily in the 2026 midterms, hoping to secure influence over the technology’s first generation of legislation – and New York City’s primary has emerged as the key battleground. AI-focused Super Pacs have raised over $100m this cycle, of which $49m has been spent so far, in dozens of congressional races across the country. Half of

stories.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives
stories.summary: Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all supposedly seen at the World Cup. But none of it is real.

stories.title: Who is Abelardo de la Espriella? From controversial lawyer to Colombia's president
stories.summary: The president-elect created his movement just 11 months ago, mirroring the model that worked in Argentina and El Salvador: patriotic talk, a firm hand against crime, and contempt for traditional politics.

stories.title: Arrest warrant issued for ex-Commissioner Avramopoulos in connection to Qatargate
stories.summary: ATHENS — Belgian authorities issued a European arrest warrant for Greece’s former European Commissioner for Migration, Dimitris Avramopoulos, in connection with the Qatargate scandal , according to two senior Greek officials involved in the judicial process who were granted anonymity as they were not authorized to speak on the issue. The Qatargate scandal broke out in December 2022 — after Avramopoulos had left the Commission — when Belgian police launched a series of raids on properties and off

stories.title: Tears, then tea: How Starmer’s dramatic resignation unfolded
stories.summary: LONDON — The Keir Starmer era is over. And Andy Burnham is preparing for power within weeks. Britain — heading for its seventh leader in a decade — has become used to the familiar spectacle of prime ministerial resignations. But the speed of events Monday still shocked even seasoned observers. Assuming no-one else can muster the 81 MP nominations required to launch a Labour leadership bid, Burnham — the popular former mayor of Greater Manchester who found a path back to parliament through a deci

stories.title: ‘Absolute nightmare’: Brexit bellwether constituencies revisited 10 years on
stories.summary: From north-east Scotland to Romford, London, what do those who spoke to the Guardian during the referendum campaign make of how it all panned out? The Guardian has revisited five bellwether constituencies we reported on during the 2016 EU referendum campaign, and asked those we spoke to at the time how they now feel about Brexit a decade on from the vote. Continue reading...

stories.title: European leaders pay tribute to Starmer as EU-UK summit is postponed
stories.summary: Ursula von der Leyen, Friedrich Merz and Volodymyr Zelenskyy say British PM helped increase security European leaders have paid tribute to Keir Starmer after he announced his resignation as the British prime minister, triggering the postponement of an upcoming EU-UK summit. The European Council president, António Costa, said: “For sure we need to postpone it, but we are reassessing the opportunity to hold this new summit … Continue reading...

stories.title: Zelenskyy pledges to ‘bring war back to Russia’ after drones swarm toward Moscow – as it happened
stories.summary: Russia intercepted 300 Ukrainian drones across the country and temporarily suspended operations from Moscow airport Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy also responded to Starmer setting out his plans to resign as Britain’s PM , telling him he would always be “a welcome guest” in Ukraine for his support to the wartorn country. stories.title: Trump claims ‘vandals’ foiled his $14m revamp of DC’s reflecting pool. What actually happened?
stories.summary: Trump’s pre-Fourth of July renovation project has endured problems with algae, peeling paint and an inflating price tag Donald Trump’s rush to repaint the Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool, a symbol of Washington DC, has hit roadblock after roadblock as the country’s 250th anniversary nears. The public has been gripped by the ill-fated $14m attempt to renovate the reflecting pool, which the US president vowed to make “beautiful” in time for this summer’s birthday celebrations at the capital. Cont

stories.title: ‘Institutional threat’: election of far-right leader raises fears for democracy in Colombia
stories.summary: Trump-admiring Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to ‘disembowel’ the left and kill criminals like ‘rats and cockroaches’ When more than 20 women accused a Colombian evangelical pastor in 2012 of sexually abusing them, the defendant’s lawyer sought to discredit the allegations by telling the court that they were “ trepadoras ” – a pejorative term meaning social climbers. He ultimately secured his client’s acquittal – although the case remains under review by the supreme court – but footage of th

stories.title: US-Iran talks laid ‘very good foundation for a successful final deal’, says Vance – as happened
stories.summary: The vice president said the move represented a ‘major milestone’ in ending Iran’s nuclear programme According to Palestinian news agency Wafa , a high school student was killed and several other civilians were injured earlier today in an Israeli attack on a civilian vehicle in Gaza City. The Gaza health ministry says at least 1,021 people have been killed in Israeli attacks since the ‘ceasefire’ between Israel and Hamas came into effect in October 2025. In a post on X, Pakistan’s prime minister,

stories.title: Canadian healthcare staff decry ‘cruel hoax’ after scam email promises paid day off
stories.summary: Unions condemn ‘insensitive’ internal cybersecurity test sent to healthcare workers in Newfoundland and Labrador For years, healthcare staff in the Canadian province of Newfoundland and Labrador have felt overworked and underappreciated. Turnover, burnout and thinning resources were pushing workers in the sector to a breaking point. So when the email titled “June Holiday” arrived in thousands of inboxes, they felt a moment of overdue joy. Continue reading...

stories.title: The Guardian view on the death of Carlo Ginzburg: a historian who taught us to think about outsiders | Editorial
stories.summary: The work of one of Italy’s greatest scholars focused on ordinary lives oppressed by power and prejudice. That approach resonates today Reflecting on the genesis of his most famous work, Carlo Ginzburg wrote that by immersing himself in the trial of a 16th-century miller burned by the Roman Inquisition, he turned a possible footnote into a book. Fifty years on, after being translated around the world, The Cheese and The Worms still stands as a supreme exemplar of historical research devoted to th

stories.title: Montreal mayor calls for end to random police checks amid racial profiling investigation
stories.summary: City’s police force faces investigation of 16 officers accused of disproportionately targeting Black and Arab residents Montreal’s mayor has called for a halt to random police checks as the city’s police force grapples with an internal investigation into racism and racial profiling by 16 officers. Mayor Soraya Martinez Ferrada told reporters last week that her husband, who is Black, had been repeatedly stopped by police while driving. Continue reading...

stories.title: Backing Ukraine, staying out of Iran and riding the Trump rollercoaster: how Starmer handled foreign affairs
stories.summary: Analysts say foreign policy was an ‘area of relative strength’ for the prime minister – but goodwill with the White House soon evaporated Keir Starmer inherited two wars and a country disconnected from the EU when he arrived in Downing Street – and that was before Donald Trump crash-landed at the White House and undermined the foundations of the UK’s most important alliance. It was a context that would have tested any prime minister, though in many respects Starmer negotiated it carefully. But l

stories.title: Judge blocks subpoenas for Walz and others over Minnesota immigration crackdown
stories.summary: Federal judge rules subpoenas linked to Trump’s immigration operation were ‘issued for unlawful reasons’ US politics live – latest updates A federal judge agreed to quash the US federal government’s subpoenas of leaders in Minnesota issued during the Trump administration ’s controversial immigration crackdown on the state earlier this year. The US Department of Justice issued subpoenas to the Minnesota governor, Tim Walz ; the attorney general, Keith Ellison; the Minneapolis mayor, Jacob Frey; a

stories.title: Andy Burnham supporters divided over who should be his chancellor
stories.summary: Briefing war breaks out between advocates for Wes Streeting and those close to Ed Miliband Andy Burnham’s supporters are divided over who should be his chancellor, with a briefing war breaking out between advocates of the former health secretary Wes Streeting and those close to the energy secretary Ed Miliband. Some of those advising the Makerfield MP are urging him to choose Streeting if he becomes prime minister, in a bid to reassure the business community and fossil fuel industry. Continue re

stories.title: Andy Burnham sworn in as an MP after Keir Starmer resigns as prime minister - as it happened
stories.summary: The prime minister said a new leader will be in place before parliament returns in September Full report: Keir Starmer to step down as prime minister two years after historic election victory Wes Streeting backs Andy Burnham to become Labour leader and PM Keir Starmer’s time as prime minister – in pictures This is from Tom Baldwin , Keir Starmer’s biographer, and head of communications for Ed Miliband when he was Labour leader. We seem to be in a strange place where Keir Starmer is being told he

stories.title: US waives Iran oil sanctions citing progress in talks
stories.summary: US officials have cited ongoing negotiations on allowing nuclear inspections and opening the Strait of Hormuz in pausing sanctions for 60 days. JD Vance said a "foundation" is in place for a final deal. More on DW.

stories.title: Why is Bulgaria threatening to veto EU's Russia sanctions?
stories.summary: Bulgarian PM Rumen Radev has vowed to veto the EU's latest sanctions against Russia because they include Russian Orthodox Patriarch Kirill and former Lukoil president Vagit Alekperov. What's behind his opposition?

stories.title: US-backed political newcomer wins Colombia election, marking country's right-wing shift
stories.summary: With nearly all votes counted, the election results polarised the country, sparking both celebrations and protests across the country.

stories.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
stories.summary: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

stories.title: Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says
stories.summary: The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children.

stories.title: Kenya minister found in contempt of court over US-backed Ebola centre
stories.summary: Aden Duale defied a High Court order to stop building work on a controversial US-funded facility.

stories.title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
stories.summary: Martha Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.

stories.title: EU-UK summit postponed as Keir Starmer resigns
stories.summary: LONDON — The EU-U.K. summit planned for next month will be postponed, the president of the European Council has said. António Costa said he hoped that Starmer’s successor as prime minister would give “continuity” to the plans to reset the cross-channel relationship. The meeting was meant to take place on July 22 to conclude talks on an agrifood agreement, an emissions trading deal, and a youth mobility scheme. The U.K. also wanted to use the meeting as a launchpad to expand talks on taking the U

stories.title: Marius Borg Høiby rape conviction renews focus in Norway on consent in digital age
stories.summary: Norway is supposedly one of world’s most gender-equal countries, yet sexual violence remains prevalent across society In many ways, the case of Marius Borg Høiby, who was sentenced to four years in prison last week after being found guilty of offences including domestic violence and two counts of rape, was exceptional. stories.title: Tell us: how is the heatwave in the UK and across Europe affecting you?
stories.summary: From sleepless nights and overheated homes to disrupted work and caring responsibilities, we want to hear how the extreme heat is affecting your daily life Temperatures are soaring across Europe, with forecasters warning that June heat records could be broken this week. Temperatures are set to reach 44C in some areas and authorities warning of risks to health, travel and public services. How are you coping with the extreme heat? Has it affected your work, health, travel plans or daily routine? A

stories.title: Brown skuas and giant petrels rarely make landfall. When they were found in WA, scientists feared ‘bad news’ for wildlife
stories.summary: Experts thought H5N1 bird flu would more likely reach Australia’s north. But an arrival from Antarctica had always been possible Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Brown skuas and giant petrels are a common sight offshore in southern Australian waters in the winter months, but they will rarely risk venturing on to land. So when two of these birds were discovered sick – on beaches a few kilometres apart on Western Australia’s southern coastline – it was a sign something

stories.title: Two children found dead in car in France as heatwave hits Europe
stories.summary: Number of countries issue alerts with sustained and rising temperatures expected to present danger to health Europe live – latest updates Two children aged four and two have been found dead in their family’s car in south-eastern France, the local prosecutor said, as a large swathe of western Europe suffers a ferocious heatwave forecast to shatter absolute temperature records. “The causes of death are yet to be determined, but the heat is the leading line of inquiry,” said Hélène Mourges, the pro

stories.title: US supreme court reinstates murder conviction in case of Etan Patz
stories.summary: Appeals court had ruled Pedro Hernandez, 64, was wrongly convicted over 1979 disappearance of New York six-year-old The US supreme court has reinstated a murder conviction in the long winding case of Etan Patz , whose 1979 disappearance at age six from New York City garnered national headlines. In a 6-3 decision on Monday, the supreme court agreed with New York prosecutors in their request to reverse a lower court ruling that had thrown out the murder conviction of Pedro Hernandez, 64, in the Pa

stories.title: Keir Starmer has quit as prime minister – what will happen next in UK politics?
stories.summary: Britain will soon have its fifth prime minister in four years. How did we get here and what challenges await Starmer’s successor? Britain is to get its fifth prime minister in four years after the current incumbent of Downing Street, Keir Starmer, announced that he would resign. It was widely expected and came after months of mounting pressure on Starmer, who led the Labour party to a landslide victory in the 2024 UK general election but who has faced months of pressure to quit from members of p

stories.title: Man charged with terrorism-linked attempted murders after Edinburgh attacks
stories.summary: Lewis Hawkes, 36, charged with five counts of attempted murder ‘aggravated by reason of having a terrorist connection’ A 36-year-old man has been charged with five counts of attempted murders “aggravated by reason of having a terrorist connection” after a series of attacks in Edinburgh last Friday. Lewis Hawkes has also been charged with assault and robbery, two counts of breach of the peace and two counts of culpable and reckless conduct, all of which were also aggravated by reason of a terrori

stories.title: Australia politics live: NSW to lower road toll cap and Queensland to promise infrastructure in today’s state budgets
stories.summary: Weekly toll cap in NSW to fall from $60 to $50 for one year. Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Good morning and welcome to our live news blog. I’m Martin Farrer with the top overnight stories and then it will be Krishani Dhanji with the main action. It’s a busy day for political types. Canberra is hurtling towards its winter break as Labor scrambles to do deals to get its major tax and NDIS reforms through the Senate. Continue reading...

stories.title: Two killed and several injured as tornado rips through southern Illinois
stories.summary: Officials say Sarita Kimble, 62, and Delores Shelton, 83, killed in Mount Vernon as several buildings destroyed Authorities in Illinois say that two older residents were killed and at least five other people were injured in a tornado that ripped through a rural county and destroyed several buildings on Sunday evening. The fatalities occurred in Mount Vernon, sheriff Jeff Bullard of Jefferson county said on Monday. He identified the victims as Sarita Kimble, 62, and Delores Shelton, 83, who were

stories.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
stories.summary: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majority A Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election that promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict. With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Esprie

stories.title: US-Iran talks strained as Trump threats spark Iranian walkout
stories.summary: Talks expected to continue for rest of the week despite disruption caused by US president’s threat to bomb Iran and kidnap negotiating team High-stakes talks between the US and Iran are expected to continue for the rest of the week in Switzerland, after a tense start that saw Iranian negotiators walk out in protest at a stream of threats issued by Donald Trump on social media. The US president had threatened to bomb Iran and even to kidnap the Iranian negotiating team unless the strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Teen accused of planning terror attack on Peter Dutton and Brisbane march was making ‘edgy joke’, court told
stories.summary: Defence barrister claims teenager was a ‘troubled kid’ but crown prosecutor says he was serious when he wrote of copying US domestic terrorists Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A teenager accused of preparing a nail bombing campaign was either a “troubled kid” who made edgy jokes or a determined terrorist in waiting, a court has been told. The teenager, who cannot be named due to his age, spent last week on trial in the Brisbane supreme court after pleading not guilty

stories.title: India news: Millions of students turn up for NEET retest
stories.summary: More than 2 million students appeared for the high-stakes NEET medical entrance exam amid high security after a question paper leak fiasco.

stories.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
stories.summary: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

stories.title: Former US Fed chair Alan Greenspan dies aged 100
stories.summary: Alan Greenspan oversaw the US Federal Reserve for nearly two decades from 1987 until 2006. Critics argue that his policies contributed to the Global Financial Crisis of 2008.

stories.title: The man who knew, but didn't say: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026)
stories.summary: Alan Greenspan’s reputation as head of the US Federal Reserve was legendary — not least because of his reputation for convoluted statements. However, the 2008/9 financial crisis took a toll on his standing.

stories.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
stories.summary: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

stories.title: How inflation undermines Nigeria's sugar tax
stories.summary: Nigeria's sugary drinks tax was meant to stop rising diabetes rates, but soaring inflation has eroded its impact. Experts say the levy is too small to change behavior and urge higher rates and reforms.

stories.title: US-Iran deal: Pakistan's diplomatic masterstroke?
stories.summary: By stepping in as mediator in the Iran war, Pakistan has managed to elevate its status from a regional player to a global diplomatic stakeholder — with a little help from China and its friends in the Gulf.

stories.title: Iran-US talks to end war beset by tension, mistrust
stories.summary: While both sides claim "encouraging progress," experts say there's a long way to go to turn the shaky diplomatic framework into a durable agreement.

stories.title: Germany and UK rebuilding ties 10 years after Brexit vote
stories.summary: Ten years after the referendum, the two countries are strengthening their ties once again. The stability of these ties is now being put to the test with Keir Starmer to be replaced as prime minister.

stories.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
stories.summary: A 30-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

stories.title: Europe sizzles under heat wave with little relief in sight
stories.summary: Germany, France, the UK and other European countries are experiencing extreme heat, with cooler weather not expected until the weekend. Several deaths were reported in France, including two children. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain’s likely next prime minister
stories.summary: Now that British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation, all eyes are on Andy Burnham. The former mayor of Manchester is seen as the favorite to succeed Starmer to lead the United Kingdom.

stories.title: Three dead in Philippines high school shooting over bullying 'grudge'
stories.summary: Police say they believe the attack was motivated by a "grudge" over bullying.

stories.title: Late singer Oliver Tree's body returned to US after helicopter crash

stories.title: Canal Saint-Martin becomes cooling spot as heatwave grips Paris
stories.summary: Paris faces extreme heat as crowds use fountains and the Canal Saint-Martin to cool off, while authorities impose alerts and safety measures.

stories.title: Giant dragon and octopus kites fill Danish skies
stories.summary: Thousands gather at Denmark's Fano International Kite Festival as giant dragon, whale and octopus kites fill the skies above the coast.

stories.title: Iran and US make progress on Hormuz despite Trump's explosive threats

stories.summary: The prohibition is intended to boost the country's economy by refining gold domestically.

stories.title: ‘There’s no jobs’: struggle and regret in a Welsh town that backed Brexit

Over the past decade, these public facilities have been joined by a public-private cybersecurity research centre and two tech firms. A new railway station opened at the site in 2015. Yet, during the Guardian’s visit to the We

stories.title: Russian strikes on eastern Ukraine kill three and injure 22
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that his forces had targeted an oil-processing facility in Russia's Tyumen region.

stories.title: Trump faces fresh bipartisan criticism on Iran deal as Vance hails peace talks

Negotiations in Lucerne between the US and Iran have already run into difficulties, after Trump wrote on Truth Social that “Ir

stories.title: The aftershocks of Brexit’s failure could be gaining strength – a fearful prospect for Ireland | Fintan O’Toole
stories.summary: On our side of the Irish Sea we have made the best of a bad job. But we saw the damage a reckless and reactionary British government could do To read more from the Brexit Vote: 10 Years On series, click here For Brexit’s true believers, Ireland will always be the spoke in the wheel that set everything off course, the green tarnish that took the shine off the golden age. Without the vengeful and malicious obstructionism of the Irish, all the promises of freedom and prosperity would have been fulf

stories.summary: Spain returns to its winning ways as Egypt break their group’s draw deadlock in the second matchday fixtures for groups G and H at the 2026 World Cup.

stories.summary: Norwegian supporters in Viking helmets and national colours packed Times Square, chanting and singing before Norway’s Group I match against Senegal.

stories.summary: Azt állítják, hogy a benzinárstop március óta 7,5-8 milliárd forint veszteséget okozott nekik.

Ten years after the Brexit referendum, the polling by the European Council on Foreign Relations (ECFR) , a thinktank, found 66% of

stories.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
stories.summary: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

stories.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
stories.summary: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. stories.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
stories.summary: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
stories.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

stories.title: Undav heroics help Germany beat Ivory Coast
stories.summary: In Germany's second 2026 World Cup game, Deniz Undav helped Germany to a comeback win against Ivory Coast in Toronto.

stories.title: Germany could scrap law banning insults against politicians
stories.summary: Germany's special law prohibiting the insulting of politicians has led to cases that many people consider absurd. Some members of the government want to abolish the law; others think there's a good reason to retain it.

stories.title: Germany's media rocked by AI scandal
stories.summary: Two German outlets were forced to delete articles that used undisclosed AI. Many fear that reliance on the technology will damage the credibility of German media.

stories.title: Why an AI company cleaned my New York City apartment for free
stories.summary: An AI company is sending free cleaners door-to-door in a bid to train the robots it hopes one day will replace them.

stories.title: At CrimeCon true crime obsessives come face-to-face with real loss
stories.summary: Victims' families say there are ways to be an ethical true-crime fan - not an exploitative one.

stories.title: Backstage at Gorillaz' epic, one-off stadium show: 'The vibe is ridiculous'
stories.summary: Damon Albarn, De La Soul and Moonchild Sannelly talk backstage as Gorillaz play their biggest show.

stories.title: Former Olympian denies vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool after arrest
stories.summary: Davey Hearn says he was simply touching the new paint at the site out of curiosity and did not remove or alter it.

stories.title: Dutch royals enjoy two big results in one World Cup day
stories.summary: There may be about 5,000 miles between the Netherlands and their former colony Curacao but members of the Dutch royal family managed to see both sides play on Saturday.

stories.title: ‘A flying White House’: President Trump unveils the new Air Force One
stories.summary: Converted from a former Qatari-owned Boeing 747, the new Air Force One will serve as a temporary presidential aircraft until Boeing delivers the long-delayed replacement fleet in 2028.

stories.title: Tight security as Indian students resit medical exam after alleged paper leak
stories.summary: Millions of candidates face biometric checks and frisking as air force deployed to secure exam papers.

stories.title: Indian cricketer, 15, hits record-breaking 50 in just 11 balls
stories.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks the record for the fastest 50-over List A half-century in history on the same day he was not included in India's one-day international squad.

stories.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
stories.summary: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

stories.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave
stories.summary: France has issued red heatwave alerts for around half the country as temperatures soar to record levels.

stories.title: Spain: PM's wife must face corruption trial, judge rules
stories.summary: A court in Madrid ruled that there is sufficient evidence against Begona Gomez, the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. The 55-year-old university director is banned from leaving the country.

stories.title: 16 dead and 12 injured following Israeli strikes, Lebanon's civil defence agency says
stories.summary: It comes less than a day after US officials said Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah had agreed to a renewed ceasefire.

stories.title: Zelenskyy issues one-week ultimatum to Lukashenka over drone-guidance equipment
stories.summary: During a media briefing in Kyiv on Friday, Zelenskyy threatened Ukraine will take down the drone relay equipment on the border if the Belarusian leader, Aliaksandr Lukashenka, doesn't do anything to address it.

stories.title: Trump threatens Hormuz tolls as ships sail on despite IRGC's closure claim
stories.summary: Iran's IRGC said it had closed the Strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes on Lebanon, but CENTCOM said 55 ships carrying 17 million barrels of oil transited unhindered. Trump meanwhile warned of US tolls if a final deal was not reached within 60 days.

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
stories.summary: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

stories.title: France bans alcohol during Fete de la Musique amid heat wave
stories.summary: France is taking additional measures as millions of people gear up for Fete de la Musique under punishing temperatures.

stories.title: Are Ukraine drones really exposing gaps in Russia's defense?
stories.summary: Once seen as almost impenetrable, Russian air defenses are now under growing strain from Ukrainian drones. A recent attack on Moscow has reignited debate over Russian vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Terror police probe suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh
stories.summary: Counterterrorism police have taken charge after a series of violent attacks in the Scottish capital that left five men injured.

stories.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
stories.summary: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

stories.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
stories.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

stories.title: Italian tourist dies in Dominican Republic resort blaze
stories.summary: Around 1,690 tourists were evacuated from a luxury resort in the village of Bayahibe after a fire broke out.

stories.title: Steve Rosenberg: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
stories.summary: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

stories.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

stories.title: Meet Osito, the rescue dog winning hearts at the World Cup
stories.summary: A rescue dog named Osito has become an unlikely World Cup star in Mexico City, attracting crowds, selfies and social media attention wherever he goes.

stories.title: Rightwing MEPs chant 'send them back' after controversial deportation plans are passed – video
stories.summary: Several lawmakers celebrated a vote in the European parliament approving plans to increase deportations of undocumented people. Chants of 'send them back' spurred other MEPs to chant back 'shame on you'. The plans could lead to ICE-style immigration measures being implemented in Europe, which have been criticised by rights groups Continue reading...

stories.title: 'All of Lebanon must burn,' Israeli minister says after IDF reports four soldiers killed
stories.summary: Israel's military said the soldiers were killed "in combat" when their tank was hit during an operation near Kfar Tebnit.

errors.error: <urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
<urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
